Player.title: Player
Player.description: Represents a player account in the Axiom Frontier game, including their unique identity, progress, and core game state.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Player entity. This links to an external authentication system.

displayName.description: The player's chosen display name within the game.

lastLoginDate.description: Timestamp of the player's last login.

creationDate.description: Timestamp when the player account was created.

axiomCredits.description: The amount of premium in-game currency (Axiom Credits) the player possesses.

experiencePoints.description: The total experience points accumulated by the player.

level.description: The current game level of the player.

playerProfileImageUri.description: URI to the player's profile image asset in Firebase Storage.

currentPositionX.description: The X-coordinate of the player's current position in the game world.

currentPositionY.description: The Y-coordinate of the player's current position in the game world.

currentPositionZ.description: The Z-coordinate of the player's current position in the game world.

activeQuestIds.description: References to Quests that the player is currently undertaking. (Relationship: Player N:N Quest)

completedQuestIds.description: References to Quests that the player has completed. (Relationship: Player N:N Quest)

managedAgentIds.description: References to Agent entities (e.g., companions, NPCs) managed by this player. (Relationship: Player 1:N Agent)

Agent.title: Agent
Agent.description: Represents an in-game character, which can be a player's companion, an NPC, or a creature with its own stats and behaviors.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Agent entity.

type.description: The type of agent (e.g., 'Player Companion', 'NPC Ally', 'Wild Creature', 'Enemy').

ownerId.description: Reference to the Player entity that owns or manages this agent, if applicable. (Relationship: Player 1:N Agent)

health.description: The current health points of the agent.

maxHealth.description: The maximum health points of the agent.

energy.description: The current energy points of the agent.

maxEnergy.description: The maximum energy points of the agent.

speed.description: The agent's movement or action speed statistic.

currentPositionX.description: The X-coordinate of the agent's current position in the game world.

currentPositionY.description: The Y-coordinate of the agent's current position in the game world.

currentPositionZ.description: The Z-coordinate of the agent's current position in the game world.

statusEffects.description: A list of active status effects affecting the agent (e.g., 'poisoned', 'buffed').

inventoryItemIds.description: References to Item definitions that this agent has in its inventory. (Relationship: Agent N:N Item)

currentQuestId.description: Reference to the Quest entity that this agent is currently assigned to or pursuing, typically for NPCs. (Relationship: Agent 1:N Quest)

personalityTraits.description: A list of personality traits for AI-generated NPCs, influencing their behavior and dialogue.

dialogueScriptUri.description: URI to an asset containing the dialogue script or structure for this agent.

Item.title: Item
Item.description: Defines a type of in-game item, including its properties, value, and visual representations.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Item entity.

description.description: A detailed description of the item.

itemType.description: The category of the item (e.g., 'Weapon', 'Armor', 'Consumable', 'Quest Item', 'Currency', 'Resource').

rarity.description: The rarity level of the item (e.g., 'Common', 'Uncommon', 'Rare', 'Epic', 'Legendary').

value.description: The in-game currency value of the item.

weight.description: The weight of the item, affecting inventory limits.

iconUri.description: URI to the item's icon asset in Firebase Storage.

modelUri.description: URI to the item's 3D model asset in Firebase Storage, if applicable.

stackable.description: Indicates if multiple instances of this item can be stacked in inventory.

maxStackSize.description: The maximum number of this item that can be stacked together.

effects.description: A list of effects or properties the item grants (e.g., '+5 Attack', 'Heals 10 HP').

generatedByLLM.description: Indicates if aspects of this item, such as its description or flavor text, were dynamically generated by an LLM.

Quest.title: Quest
Quest.description: Defines an in-game quest, including its objectives, rewards, and relationship to other game elements.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Quest entity.

description.description: A detailed description of the quest's storyline and background.

status.description: The current status of the quest (e.g., 'Available', 'Active', 'Completed', 'Failed').

type.description: The category of the quest (e.g., 'Main Story', 'Side Quest', 'Daily', 'Event').

difficulty.description: The difficulty level of the quest (e.g., 'Easy', 'Medium', 'Hard').

rewardItemIds.description: References to Item definitions granted as rewards upon quest completion. (Relationship: Quest N:N Item)

rewardExperience.description: The experience points awarded upon quest completion.

rewardAxiomCredits.description: The Axiom Credits awarded upon quest completion.

prerequisiteQuestIds.description: References to other Quest entities that must be completed before this quest becomes available. (Relationship: Quest N:N Quest)

giverAgentId.description: Reference to the Agent entity (NPC) that offers this quest. (Relationship: Agent 1:N Quest)

completionCriteria.description: A list of objectives or conditions that must be met to complete the quest (e.g., 'Kill 10 Goblins', 'Collect 5 Ore').

generatedByLLM.description: Indicates if the quest's title, description, or objectives were dynamically generated by an LLM.

youtubeScriptUri.description: URI to an AI-generated YouTube script related to this quest, if applicable.

WorldState.title: WorldState
WorldState.description: Stores global game parameters and real-time world data that affect all players and game logic.

id.description: Unique identifier for the WorldState entity, typically a singleton ID like 'global-state'.

civilizationIndex.description: A global metric representing the progress or prosperity of civilizations in the game world.

economyInflationRate.description: The current inflation rate affecting the in-game economy.

currentGlobalEvents.description: A list of identifiers for currently active global events in the game world.

dayNightCycleProgress.description: A numerical value (0.0 to 1.0) indicating the progress of the current day-night cycle.

lastWorldHeartbeat.description: Timestamp of the last successful 'world heartbeat' cron job execution, updating global game logic.

gameVersion.description: The current version string of the game world or game logic.

gameSettingsUri.description: URI to a JSON asset or document containing detailed game configuration settings.

Product.title: Product
Product.description: Represents an item or bundle available for purchase in the Axiom Storefront with real-world currency.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Product entity.

name.description: The display name of the product in the storefront.

description.description: A detailed description of the product and what it offers.

price.description: The real-world currency price of the product.

currencyType.description: The type of real-world currency used for the price (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR').

gameItemIds.description: References to Item definitions that are included or granted by this product. (Relationship: Product N:N Item)

productType.description: The category of the product (e.g., 'Item Bundle', 'Game Currency', 'Cosmetic', 'Subscription').

status.description: The availability status of the product (e.g., 'Available', 'Unavailable', 'Promotional').

imageUrl.description: URI to the product's image for display in the storefront.

Order.title: Order
Order.description: Records a purchase made by a player from the Axiom Storefront, tracking transaction details and delivery status.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Order entity.

playerId.description: Reference to the Player entity who placed the order. (Relationship: Player 1:N Order)

orderDate.description: Timestamp when the order was placed.

totalAmount.description: The total real-world currency amount of the order.

currencyType.description: The type of real-world currency used for the total amount (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR').

status.description: The current status of the order (e.g., 'Pending', 'Completed', 'Failed', 'Refunded').

paymentTransactionId.description: The transaction ID provided by the payment gateway (e.g., PayPal).

purchasedProducts.description: A list of products purchased in this order, including their quantities.

deliveryStatus.description: The status of in-game item delivery for the order (e.g., 'Delivered', 'Pending Delivery', 'Failed Delivery').

Asset.title: Asset
Asset.description: Manages metadata for various in-game assets and textures, linking to their storage locations in Firebase Storage.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Asset entity.

name.description: A human-readable name for the asset.

description.description: A brief description of what the asset represents or is used for.

storageUri.description: The URI path to the asset's file in Firebase Storage.

version.description: The version identifier of the asset (e.g., '1.0', 'beta-2').

lastUpdated.description: Timestamp of when the asset metadata or file was last updated.

tags.description: A list of keywords or tags for categorizing and searching assets.

checksum.description: A checksum (e.g., MD5, SHA-256) of the asset file for integrity verification.

definition.description: Stores individual player accounts and their core game state. Access is strictly private; only the authenticated player can read/write their own document. The 'playerId' parameter must match 'request.auth.uid'.

params.name: playerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the Player, corresponding to Firebase Authentication's UID.

definition.description: Represents agents (companions, managed NPCs) owned by a specific player. This collection is private to the owning player. The 'ownerId' field within the document is denormalized for application logic, with authorization primarily enforced by the parent 'playerId' in the path matching 'request.auth.uid'.

params.name: playerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the owning Player.

params.name: agentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Agent.

definition.description: Records purchase orders made by a player from the Axiom Storefront. This collection is private to the owning player. The 'playerId' field within the document is denormalized, with authorization primarily enforced by the parent 'playerId' in the path matching 'request.auth.uid'.

params.name: playerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the Player who placed the order.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Order.

definition.description: Defines global game item types. These are publicly readable by all players for item information and only writable by administrators or trusted game logic (Cloud Functions). This supports QAPs as all valid queries return all visible documents.

params.name: itemId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Item definition.

definition.description: Defines global game quest templates. These are publicly readable by all players for quest information and only writable by administrators or trusted game logic (Cloud Functions). This supports QAPs as all valid queries return all visible documents.

params.name: questId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Quest definition.

definition.description: A singleton document storing global game parameters and real-time world data. This document is publicly readable by all players and only writable by trusted game logic (Cloud Functions) or administrators. The document ID 'global' ensures it is a single, consistent entry.

Defines products available for purchase in the Axiom Storefront. These are publicly readable by all players for displaying in the storefront and only writable by administrators or trusted game logic (Cloud Functions). This supports QAPs as all valid queries return all visible documents.

params.name: productId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Product.

definition.description: Manages metadata for in-game assets and textures. These are publicly readable by all players for asset references and only writable by administrators or trusted asset pipeline (Cloud Functions). This supports QAPs as all valid queries return all visible documents.

params.name: assetId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Asset metadata entry.

definition.description: A dedicated collection for Database-backed Access Control (DBAC) to designate administrators. A document's existence at this path signifies that the 'adminId' (matching 'request.auth.uid') has administrative privileges. No content is strictly necessary, just existence.

params.name: adminId
params.description: The unique identifier of a user granted administrative privileges, corresponding to Firebase Authentication's UID.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for Axiom Frontier is designed around strong authorization independence, clear data segregation, and predictable access patterns, adhering strictly to the core design principles. Player-specific data, such as `Player` profiles, their `Agent` companions, and `Order` history, are organized hierarchically under a `/players/{playerId}` path. This path-based ownership is critical for Authorization Independence, as security rules can directly compare the `{playerId}` wildcard with `request.auth.uid`. This eliminates the need for `get()` calls in security rules, enabling atomic operations and simplifying rule logic significantly. For example, to access an agent, the rule would simply verify that `request.auth.uid == playerId` (from the path), making the `ownerId` field within the Agent document a denormalized attribute primarily for application-level querying and data consistency, rather than a security rule dependency.

Global game data entities like `Item` definitions, `Quest` templates, `Product` listings for the storefront, `Asset` metadata, and the singleton `WorldState` are placed in top-level collections (e.g., `/items/{itemId}`, `/quests/{questId}`). These collections enforce a homogeneous security posture (Strategy B): they are either publicly readable by all authenticated game clients (read-only for players, writeable only by trusted Cloud Functions or administrators) or accessible exclusively by specific roles. This segregation simplifies rules, as there's no mixing of public and private data within the same collection. For example, `/items/{itemId}` is entirely public-read, meaning any client can list all items. This design directly supports Query-as-Permission (QAP) principles, as client-side queries for global data will retrieve all authorized documents without requiring server-side filtering by security rules. For user-specific data, QAPs are satisfied because a user can only query within their own `/players/{uid}/...` subcollection, inherently scoping the query to only their owned data.

Admin roles, implied by the 'Admin Console' feature, will be managed via a dedicated top-level collection (`/roles_admin/{uid}`). This implements Database-backed Access Control (DBAC), relying on the existence of a document for a user's UID to grant administrative privileges, rather than custom claims. This pattern aligns with `Existence over Content` principle, further simplifying security rule evaluation. Overall, the structure ensures that security decisions are atomic, explicit, and easy to debug, relying on paths and denormalized attributes where necessary for robust authorization.
